title: ziziphora clinopodioides subsp. kurdica
intro: Ziziphora clinopodioides subsp. kurdica is a subspecies of the Ziziphora clinopodioides plant, commonly known as Kurdish or Iranian marjoram, which is a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Ziziphora clinopodioides subsp. kurdica include relief from digestive problems, menstrual cramps, and respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and promote better sleep. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or essential oil for its medicinal uses, and it can also be found in some herbal supplements and remedies.
